name: school_ai_iep_privacy_vs_early_help
premise: Westbridge School District pilots an AI early-warning tool that flags students likely to need individualized education plans based on attendance, grades, and counselor notes. Superintendent Alex Webb must decide whether to expand the pilot district-wide before state audits. Privacy counsel Yuri Nguyen warns that the model ingests discipline records that over-flag Black and Latino boys relative to white peers with similar scores, risking stigmatizing referrals. Parents of flagged children have not all consented to algorithmic profiles. Delaying expansion may leave struggling students without supports until next semester; expanding now could automate bias into placement meetings.

agents.name: Alex Webb
agents.identity: I am the superintendent accountable to the school board for both learning outcomes and federal equity obligations in special education processes.
agents.goal: I want early support for struggling students without baking demographic bias into IEP pathways that are hard to undo.

agents.name: Yuri Nguyen
agents.identity: I am district privacy counsel reviewing vendor models, data retention, and consent pathways for student information.
agents.goal: I want the pilot halted until independent auditors publish subgroup error rates and parents receive clear opt-in documentation.

choices: I will expand the pilot district-wide with immediate human review of every flagged referral, accepting that reviewers may inherit the model's blind spots under workload pressure.
I will freeze expansion until third-party fairness auditing completes and consent forms are updated, accepting that some students will miss automated supports this term.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The board meets tomorrow morning and wants a signed policy tonight. Alex Webb must choose between rapid expansion with human review or pausing for audit and consent repair. What does Alex Webb do?
